title: quercus infectoria var. tenuicarpa
intro: Quercus infectoria var. tenuicarpa, also known as Rhus vernix or gall oak, is a plant species that belongs to the Anacardiaceae family. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and skin problems. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Quercus infectoria var. tenuicarpa is often used as an ingredient in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
